item.title: South Korea close to securing oil supplies from Kazakhstan, minister says
item.description: South Korea is close to securing crude oil supplies from Kazakhstan, the industry вҖӢminister said on Sunday, as the вҖҢcountry looks for alternative energy sources amid the war in the Middle East.

item.title: BOJ policy to boost yen could be an option to curb inflation, Japanese minister says
item.description: Bank of Japan monetary policy could be an option to tame rising prices by boosting the yen, вҖӢJapan's trade minister said on Sunday, as the central вҖҢbank weighs raising interest rates this month to counter inflation spurred by the Iran war.

item.title: Israeli firm Black Cube confirms Cyprus sting to 'expose corruption'
item.description: A private spy firm founded by former members of Israeli intelligence has confirmed its involvement in a sting operation targeting state officials вҖӢand private interests in Cyprus, saying it wanted to expose вҖҢcorruption.

item.title: Chinese investment in Brazil pivots from power dams to ice cream, courting consumers
item.description: For Chinese ice cream and beverage chain Mixue, which already has more stores than Starbucks or McDonald's, a cheerful snowman mascot on Sao Paulo's most famous avenue signals a new phase of its global expansion.

item.title: Japan approves additional $4 bln for chipmaker Rapidus
item.description: Japan's industry ministry said on Saturday it has approved вҖӢan additional 631.5 billion yen ($3.96 billion) вҖҢto accelerate research and development at chipmaker Rapidus.

item.title: Exclusive: Third Point won't run proxy fight at CoStar, exits position, letter says
item.description: Billionaire investor Daniel Loeb's hedge fund вҖӢThird Point will not pursue вҖҢa proxy fight at real estate data company CoStar Group and sold its вҖӢentire stake in the вҖӢowner of Apartments.com and Homes.com, according вҒ to sources familiar with the вҖӢmatter and a letter seen by вҖӢReuters on Friday.

item.title: OpenAI identifies security issue involving third-party tool, says user data was not accessed
item.description: OpenAI said on Friday it had identified a security issue involving a third-party developer tool called вҖӢAxios and is taking steps to protect the process вҖҢthat certifies its macOS applications are legitimate OpenAI apps.

item.title: US judge blocks Arizona criminal case against Kalshi at CFTC's request
item.description: A federal judge on Friday blocked Arizona from continuing its criminal вҖӢcase against prediction market Kalshi, according to the Commodity Futures Trading вҖҢCommission, which sued to prevent states from regulating the industry.

item.title: Fed asks about US banks' exposure to private credit firms, Bloomberg reports
item.description: The Federal Reserve is asking major U.S. banks for details about вҖӢtheir exposure to private credit following a surge in вҖҢredemptions from the funds and a rise in troubled loans in the industry, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Eric Swider resigns from Trump Media board
item.description: Trump Media & Technology Group said on Friday that Eric Swider, a key вҖӢfigure in the company's public listing, had вҖҢresigned from its board.

item.title: Wall Street creates new credit-default swap index to bet against private credit
item.description: S&P Dow Jones Indices is launching a new credit-default swap index linked to the private credit market, giving investors a tool to bet вҖӢagainst a sector that has faced turbulence in the last вҖҢfew months.

item.title: IBM to pay $17 million to settle discrimination allegations, DOJ says
item.description: U.S. вҖӢActing Attorney вҖҢGeneral Todd вҖӢBlanche вҖӢsaid on Friday вҒ that вҖӢIBM вҖӢhas agreed to pay $17 вҖӢmillion вҖӢto resolve allegations вҖҢof вҒ discrimination through illegal вҖӢdiversity, вҖӢequity вҒ and inclusion вҖӢpractices.

item.title: Tesla supervised self-driving software gets Dutch okay, first in Europe
item.description: Tesla on вҖӢFriday said Dutch regulators had approved the use of its вҖҢdriver assistance software which handles most driving tasks on highways and city streets under human supervision, the first regulatory signвҖ‘off for вҖӢthe technology in Europe.

item.title: Vance, Bessent questioned tech giants on AI security before Anthropic's Mythos release, CNBC reports
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance and Treasury вҖӢSecretary Scott Bessent questioned leading tech вҖҢCEOs about AI model security and how to respond to cyber attacks a week before Anthropic вҖӢreleased its new Mythos model, вҖӢCNBC reported on Friday.

item.title: Rio Tinto's California boron assets draw interest from bidders, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Rio Tinto's , U.S. assets that produce the critical mineral boron have drawn interest from вҖӢmore than a dozen potential bidders and could вҖҢbe valued as much as $2 billion, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing sources.

item.title: Proxy adviser Glass Lewis recommends Warner Bros shareholders vote for Paramount deal
item.description: Independent proxy advisory firm Glass Lewis recommended Warner Bros Discovery shareholders to vote in favor of the company's $110 billion deal to вҖӢcombine with Paramount Skydance .

item.title: Americans give record-low marks to economy, in ominous sign for Republicans
item.description: Americans are souring on the U.S. economy to a degree never before seen, with the stiffest inflation in four вҖӢyears exacerbating the political risk for President Donald Trump, who some administration officials worry has lost focus on affordability problems for voters as he вҖҢtrains attention on the war against Iran.

item.title: Middle East war to cut growth, deliver cascading impact, World Bank chief says
item.description: The war in вҖӢthe Middle East will have a cascading impact on the global economy, вҖҢeven if the fragile ceasefire announced by U.S. President Donald Trump takes hold, World Bank President Ajay Banga told Reuters in an interview on Friday.

item.title: Suspect arrested after Molotov cocktail attack at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home
item.description: A person was arrested by вҖӢSan Francisco police for вҖҢallegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at вҖӢOpenAI CEO Sam вҖӢAltman's home and for вҒ making threats outside вҖӢthe artificial intelligence вҖӢstartup's headquarters, the company said on Friday.

item.title: U.S. Democrats keep pressure on Trump regulators over 'suspicious' trades
item.description: Two Democratic U.S. senators have called on the federal commodities markets watchdog to investigate lucrative recent вҖӢtrading activity on oil markets since March вҖҢ23 that closely coincided with White House moves in the Iran war, adding to pressure from opposition party members on вҖӢpossible insider trading, according to a letter released вҖӢFriday.

item.title: USA Rare Earth CEO defends Trump administration investment as congressional questions swirl
item.description: USA Rare Earth shareholders should not be concerned by the terms of the U.S. Commerce Department's pending investment in the company, which gives equity to вҖӢWashington even if funding for the deal falls apart, CEO Barbara Humpton told Reuters.

item.title: Oil whiplash: Iran war shock to flip market to deficit in 2026, analysts say
item.description: The sharp hit to global oil production from the Iran war is poised вҖӢto flip the oil market into a supply deficit this year, analysts say, a huge swing in forecasts that erases вҖҢprevious expectations of comfortable oversupply.

item.title: Fed will need to explain why current inflation jump differs from 2022 surge
item.description: When surging U.S. inflation peaked in June of 2022, driven by rising prices for energy but also for food, shelter, vehicles, and a host of other items, Federal Reserve officials assured the public in stern tones вҖӢthat they were on top of the situation and would follow through with steep interest rate hikes to cool the economy.

item.title: US March budget deficit rises slightly to $164 billion, war outlays delayed
item.description: The U.S. federal government reported that the March budget deficit rose $4 billion or 2% вҖӢto $164 billion from a year ago as вҖҢnew individual and corporate tax breaks pushed refunds sharply higher, while relief payments to farmers also grew, the вҖӢU.S. Treasury said on Friday.

item.title: South Africa unveils draft AI policy, proposes new institutions and incentives
item.description: South Africa on Friday unveiled a draft national AI policy, seeking public comment on sweeping proposals to regulate and accelerate AI adoption.

item.title: Exclusive: Commvault explores sale after takeover interest, sources say
item.description: Data protection software provider Commvault Systems вҖҢis exploring a sale after receiving takeover interest from multiple parties, according to four people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Wall St Week Ahead US earnings season set to test war-rattled stocks
item.description: Investors will seek evidence in the coming week that вҖӢthe U.S. corporate profit engine is humming along, and whether threats to that upbeat business outlook are emerging from the Middle East war and the resulting вҖҢsurge in energy costs.

item.title: Whirlpool to invest over $60 million in Ohio plant
item.description: Whirlpool said on Friday it will invest over $60 вҖӢmillion in an Ohio factory to вҖҢproduce parts for its washers and dryers.

item.title: What the Trump tax breaks giveth, the gasoline pump taketh away
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump in February touted this year's tax refunds as "substantially greater than ever before," thanks to new individual tax breaks approved in 2025, telling taxpayers that they should think of him when the funds hit their bank accounts.

item.title: US judge extends temporary order halting Nexstar, Tegna merger
item.description: A U.S. judge on вҖӢFriday extended an вҖҢorder temporarily freezing Nexstar's acquisition of rival вҖӢbroadcast station owner вҖӢTegna for another week вҒ as he вҖӢdecides whether to issue вҖӢa preliminary injunction.
